title: IS-Sahel Launches Motorcycle Attack on FAMa Patrol North of Ménaka
detail: Islamic State – Wilayat Sahel (IS-Sahel) fighters mounted on motorcycles attacked a Malian Armed Forces (FAMa) patrol in the Inkadevan area, north of Ménaka in eastern Mali. Militants engaged the patrol with fire before withdrawing. FAMa helicopters subsequently conducted airstrikes against the retreating attackers in the Tinfadimat area. Exact casualties from either engagement were not confirmed in open-source reporting. The attack followed FAMa's large airstrike on an IS-Sahel base in the same region on March 26, suggesting continued high operational tempo by both sides in the Ménaka theater.

sources.name: Pravda Mali — FAMa Ménaka patrol engagement

title: JNIM Assassinates Youth Organization Leader in Tonka, Timbuktu Region
detail: Armed JNIM fighters on motorcycles shot and killed Amadou Baby (known as 'Marco'), deputy president of a local youth association, outside his store in Tonka municipality, Timbuktu Region, at approximately 8:00 PM local time. JNIM accused the organization of collaborating with military forces and facilitating arrests of JNIM fighters. Several other association members had fled the area following prior JNIM threats; Baby remained despite warnings. The targeted killing follows JNIM's documented pattern of eliminating civil society leaders perceived as pro-government in northern Mali's Timbuktu and Gao regions. Such assassinations serve both to punish actual or alleged collaboration and to coerce the civilian population into non-cooperation with state security forces.

sources.name: Pravda Burkina Faso — Tonka assassination report
